name: Coagulation factor V antigen
description: Immunological quantity of factor V protein ; distinct from factor V activity (functional)

concept.conceptName: Coagulation factor V Ag [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay
Coagulation factor V Ag actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay

en.name: Coagulation factor V antigen
en.shortDescription: Immunological quantity of factor V protein ; distinct from factor V activity (functional)
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Factor V antigen is the **immunological measurement of the factor V protein concentration** in plasma — what this concept set measures. It quantifies how much factor V protein is present, irrespective of whether the molecule is functionally active [1]. The result is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, produced on platelet-poor plasma by immunoassay [1, 2]. Results are reported either as a percentage of normal plasma concentration or in IU/mL [3]. **Note: activity vs antigen.** This concept set covers **antigen** only — the immunological mass of the factor V protein. The functional capacity of the protein in the clotting cascade is captured by a separate concept set, "Coagulation factor V activity". The two values can diverge in qualitative deficits where antigen is normal but activity is reduced [1] — they are different study variables and must not be merged. ## Included Concepts The set contains two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, both measuring factor V antigenic concentration in platelet-poor plasma by immunoassay, differing only by the result representation: - *3027396 / 3194-8 — Coagulation factor V Ag [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)*: result expressed in **U/mL** or **IU/mL**. - *3023945 / 3195-5 — Coagulation factor V Ag actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay (LOINC)*: result expressed as a **percentage** of pooled normal plasma antigenic concentration. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: - For `3194-8` (Units/volume): **`[arb'U]/mL`** (concept 8980 — arbitrary unit per millilitre, equivalent to IU/mL). - For `3195-5` (actual/normal): **`%`** (concept 8554 — percent). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The following LOINC concepts that exist for factor V are deliberately not part of this set (they belong to other concept sets): - **Factor V activity** (`3005757 / 3193-0`, `3006924 / 3192-2`): captured by "Coagulation factor V activity". - **Factor V inhibitor** (`3004057 / 3191-4`, `21494686 / 81124-0`): rare bleeding disorder workup, separate scope. - **Factor V activity and inhibitor panel** (`36031832 / 96458-5`): panel grouping concept, not a measurement result. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source explicitly reports "factor V antigen", "factor V Ag", "facteur V antigène" with the result expressed as a percentage of normal: `3023945 / 3195-5 — Coagulation factor V Ag actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay`. - **Source reports IU/mL or U/mL for factor V antigen**: map to `3027396 / 3194-8 — Coagulation factor V Ag [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay`. - **Source reports a single "factor V" without specifying activity vs antigen**: by default, map to the "Coagulation factor V activity" concept set — the activity assay is the routine clinical measurement; antigen is a specialty test. Only map to this antigen concept set when the source unambiguously labels the result as "Ag" / "antigen" / "antigène". - **Do not confuse with**: factor V activity (different concept set), factor V Leiden genotype (genetic test, not immunoassay), factor V inhibitor titre. ## References [1] Asselta R, Tenchini ML, Duga S. Inherited defects of coagulation factor V: the hemorrhagic side. *J Thromb Haemost*. 2006;4(1):26-34. Available: https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1538-7836.2005.01590.x [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 3194-8 — Coagulation factor V Ag [Units/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay. Available: https://loinc.org/3194-8/ [3] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
